findings.line: It hurt less than she had feared and more.
findings.issue: Paragraph-final emotional summary that closes the beat after Jaina's answer instead of leaving it on the spoken line.

audits.registerSummary: Jaina thinks in urgent logistical triage, magical mechanism, and concrete street-level consequences. She can compress observations into tactical shorthand, but she does not naturally zoom out into detached civic-analysis phrasing while riding a clock.

findings.line: Stratholme held together by habit and traffic law.
findings.povMismatch: Jaina can clock orderly movement, but 'traffic law' is detached explanatory diction that sits outside her pressured, in-body register here.

audits.secondSweep: Re-read dialogue-heavy exchanges and tactical/inferential paragraphs; only the abstract stand-in below failed the cash-out test once the local carrier check was applied.

findings.line: She staggered back into a lane with too many answers in it.
findings.failedQuestion: What does this cash out to?
findings.missingPayload: The lane is visibly full of bread, carts, and household proof that the contamination has already crossed into daily life.
findings.reason: Placeholder noun stand-in ('answers') asks the reader to supply the concrete street evidence instead of naming or omitting it.
findings.suggestedFix: She staggered back into the lane.

summary.verdictReason: Three mild residual pass-b issues remain, all locally fixable without adding new content or changing scene logic.
